title: auto-psych: Automating the science of mind using agent-driven theory discovery and experimentation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26460v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: AI-based scientific automation is increasingly possible by using agents to generate hypotheses, design experiments, and analyze data. Data collection is a major bottleneck in this pipeline, however. Psychology, and computational cognitive science in particular, is well-positioned to benefit from AI experimentation because theories are often represented as code and crowdsourcing platforms enable programmatic human data collection at scale. Here, we apply automated discovery techniques to the project of generating theories in computational cognitive science, with an agent-based system collecting human data independently through crowdsourced survey experiments. As a testbed, we use a classic case study from cognitive psychology: judging which sequences of coin flips seem subjectively more random. Our system, auto-psych, uses nested agent-based discovery loops to generate explanatory theories of human behavior. The inner loop conjectures, fi

translated_title: auto-psych：利用智能体驱动的理论发现与实验自动化心智科学
translated_abstract: 基于人工智能的科学自动化正日益成为可能，通过使用智能体来生成假设、设计实验和分析数据。然而，数据收集是这一流程中的主要瓶颈。心理学，尤其是计算认知科学，因其理论常以代码形式呈现，且众包平台能实现大规模程序化人类数据收集，因此具备从AI实验中获益的独特优势。在此，我们将自动化发现技术应用于计算认知科学中的理论生成项目，通过基于智能体的系统独立收集众包调查实验的人类数据。作为测试平台，我们采用了认知心理学中的一个经典案例：判断哪些硬币抛掷序列在主观上显得更随机。我们的系统auto-psych利用嵌套的基于智能体的发现循环来生成人类行为的解释性理论。内层循环进行猜想、拟合。
tldr: 本文提出了一个名为auto-psych的自动化系统，它通过嵌套的智能体驱动发现循环，结合众包实验自主收集人类数据，实现了对计算认知科学中人类行为（如随机性判断）解释性理论的自动生成。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces auto-psych, an automated system that uses nested agent-driven discovery loops and crowdsourced experiments to autonomously generate explanatory theories of human behavior, such as randomness judgments, in computational cognitive science.
